claim: A U.S. F-15 fighter jet was shot down over Iranian territory, with Iranian State TV urging citizens to capture the crew.
U.S. officials confirmed the destruction of Iran's air force, navy, and missile capability is either already achieved or well underway.
The U.S. is deploying thousands of additional troops to the Middle East to press the military offensive forward.
Trump threatened to strike every Iranian power plant if a deal isn't reached.
A 24-hour State Department task force has been established to assist U.S. citizens amid evacuations across the Gulf region.
Iran's Foreign Minister stated Iran is prepared for six more months of war.
U.S. intelligence suggests approximately half of Iran's missile launchers remain intact more than a month into the conflict.
Iranian forces launched drone attacks on Kuwaiti desalination plants and oil refineries.
The United Arab Emirates closed gas facilities following a reported fire caused by debris from an intercepted missile.
The White House passed a fifteen-point U.S. peace proposal through Pakistan encompassing reopening the Strait of Hormuz, missile limits, sanctions relief, and rollback on Iran's nuclear program.
Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in U.S. and Israeli strikes on February 28.
The Assembly of Experts appointed Ali Khamenei's son, Mojtaba Khamenei, as successor to the Supreme Leader.
Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian faced fierce political backlash after signaling conditional willingness to end the war.
Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr was appointed as the new chief of the Supreme National Security Council, replacing Ali Larijani who was killed in an Israeli strike.
Severe shortages and soaring prices for key medicines persisted in Iran, with some insulin brands reaching approximately $46.70.
Dozens of IRGC-linked money changers were arrested in the United Arab Emirates, dealing serious blows to Tehran's sanctions-evasion network.
Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said Tehran was reviewing the U.S. peace proposal but insisted it did not intend to negotiate.
The U.S. conflict with Iran is expected to conclude in two to three weeks according to U.S. officials.
Multiple countries are experiencing energy crisis ripple effects globally from the Iran conflict.
Iran's mission to the United Nations dismissed Trump's Wednesday speech as 'ignorance, not strength.'
